channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: The Samsung S99H is one of the best OLED TVs I've ever tested, even if it does feature a controversial design
item.description: Samsung's latest flagship OLED TV, the S99H, delivers on picture and performance, but features a controversial design change.

item.title: The Samsung S95H is one of the best OLED TVs I've ever tested, even if it does feature a controversial design
item.description: Samsung's latest flagship OLED TV, the S95H, delivers on picture and performance, but features a controversial design change.

item.title: AWS Billing Glitch Hits Customers With Billion-Dollar Fees
item.description: An error with the cloud computing giant’s billing operation caused some customers’ monthly bills to rise from a few cents to billions of dollars.

item.title: Apple and Google ordered to purge ‘nudify’ apps from App Stores
item.description: In letters sent to Apple and Google, San Francisco City Attorney David Chiu said that both companies have long been aware that they are hosting apps in violation of state law.

item.title: TikTok is testing an AI likeness detection tool
item.description: TikTok is starting to test an opt-in tool that scans for AI likenesses and lets creators report them to the company, as spotted by social media consultant Matt Navarra. The tool is initially being tested with "some" US creators, TikTok US spokesperson Zachary Kizer tells The Verge. YouTube has been working on a similar tool […]

item.title: Hackers breached DHS after alarms were twice ruled 'false positives'
item.description: DHS analysts twice ruled intrusion alerts on its HSIN network "false positives", allowing hackers to have weeks of unintended access on the platform.

item.title: Nuclear startup Valar Atomics in talks to raise new funding at $6B valuation
item.description: The potential deal highlights a growing trend of complex, multi-stage funding rounds that mask true entry prices.

item.title: What Cities From Chicago to Washington, DC, Look Like Under a Blanket of Wildfire Smoke
item.description: The US is home to some of the most polluted air on the planet as smoke from 120 out-of-control Canadian wildfires drifts across the border.

item.title: Pebble founder Eric Migicovsky says his 30-day warranty is all about trust
item.description: Pebble founder Eric Migicovsky says buyers of its new e-paper smartwatches should know what they're signing up for and trust Pebble to make things right if they run into issues, despite the short warranty. "I think the most important thing is trust," Migicovsky told me in an interview this week. "Do people trust the product […]

item.title: Zoox issues software recall because its robotaxis may be confused by smoke
item.description: The NHTSA recently called for autonomous vehicle companies to improve how cars respond in emergency situations.

item.title: This super-bright Xgimi Horizon smart projector packs built-in Harman Kardon audio — and it’s $370 off
item.description: Skip the 4K tax with this 1080p Android TV projector featuring 2200 ANSI lumens and Harman Kardon speakers for under $530.

item.title: Healthier recipes for your Ninja Creami — how I eliminated artificial sweeteners and still made the richest, creamiest ice cream of the summer
item.description: The main reason to buy a Ninja Creami is so that you can make frozen treats that are healthier than what you can buy in a store.

item.title: Nike nearly ended an investigation into its DEI practices—until Trump took office
item.description: A New York Times report revealed that the EEOC chose not to settle with Nike—a decision that could have major consequences for diversity programs in the workplace. When the Equal Employment Opportunity Commission opened an investigation into Nike’s diversity practices, it was unusual for a number of reasons. The federal agency’s mission has long been to enforce anti-discrimination laws, which involved pursuing claims brought by workers who had faced bias over their race or gender or other aspects of their identity. The Nike case—which suggested the company had discriminated against white empl...

item.title: The Odyssey in IMAX is worth a long journey of your own
item.description: The Odyssey in IMAX is worth a long journey of your own.

item.title: Agents think in milliseconds, legacy infrastructure doesn't. LinkedIn, Walmart and Zendesk shared how they closed the gap at VB Transform 2026
item.description: Legacy infrastructure, not the models themselves, is what's actually slowing AI agents down. That was the shared conclusion of three infrastructure leaders — from LinkedIn, Walmart, and Zendesk — at VB Transform 2026.The panel brought together Animesh Singh, senior director of AI platform and infrastructure at LinkedIn, Desiree Gosby, SVP of corporate technology services and technology strategy at Walmart, and Sami Ghoche, VP of applied AI at Zendesk, each describing what actually broke when they moved agents from pilot to production. Each arrived at the same conclusion from a different starti...

item.title: How an old meme is giving Claire’s some much-needed attention
item.description: No, you can’t actually get a lobotomy there, but the long-suffering jewelry and accessories retailer is having fun with the idea anyway. And why not? A staple of the millennial mall experience, Claire’s is known as the place where teenagers can score whimsical jewelry and get pierced. But on social media, the accessory retailer is also a dentist office, a surgical procedure room, and a go-to stop for any medical needs....

item.title: What’s next in Apple’s legal battle with OpenAI
item.description: Apple's lawsuit against OpenAI is just getting started. Here's what could happen next and what it means for OpenAI's hardware ambitions.

item.title: Apple Music and Apple One prices rise for the first time in years
item.description: Apple is blaming an Apple Music price hike on "rising licensing costs."

item.title: Polymarket’s Corporate Structure Is a Mystery—Even to Some of Its Former Employees
item.description: The prediction market’s Panamanian operation seems odd, even for a company whose CEO had his apartment raided by FBI agents.

item.title: Is the Apple TV 4K still worth buying after the price hike?
item.description: Apple's streaming box is now much more expensive than comparable alternatives. Should you opt for a Fire TV or Roku instead?

item.title: What Zelensky risks by firing Ukraine’s tech-minded defense minister
item.description: Mykhailo Fedorov helped turn drones, software, and faster procurement into military advantages. His removal could slow the reforms Ukraine needs to compete with Russia. On July 15, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky dismissed Defense Minister Mykhailo Fedorov, the official most closely identified with Ukraine’s much-lauded effort to turn drones, software, and commercial technology into military advantage. The move could have implications not just for Kyiv, but also for Washington....

item.title: Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra appears online with Messis face and a $13,000 price tag
item.description: A week before its expected launch, a company called Caviar has listed the Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra for sale online, with a twist.

item.title: Apple Music is getting a price hike
item.description: Apple Music is more expensive now. In the US, an individual plan now costs $11.99 per month, a $1 bump up from the previous $10.99 price. A family plan now costs $19.99 per month, up from $16.99, and a student plan costs $6.99 per month, up from $5.99. Apple, in a statement to Music Business […]

item.title: Here are the Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8 and Z Fold 8 camera and battery specs, apparently
item.description: Camera upgrades in the Z Flip 8, Fold 8 and Fold 8 Ultra appear to be slim.
